result:
1. Trusts client `X-User-Id` header for identity — Section 2 rule 1, Section 5. Client-supplied `x-user-id` is stripped at edge; even if not, it is forbidden as identity source. Attacker sets any user. Fix: read `req.user.sub` (or `X-Internal-User-Id` set after `auth_jwt`); remove `req.headers['x-user-id']`.

2. No authentication/authorization on route — Section 3, Section 5. Handler registered with `app.post` bypasses pipeline expectations; no check that caller's JWT `org` claim matches `:org_id`, no role check for transfer. Cross-org fund movement possible. Fix: mount under the authed router, assert `req.user.org === req.params.org_id` (or membership lookup) and required role in `req.user.roles`.

3. No input validation on body — Section 12 (correctness), Section 2 (trust boundary). `from`, `to`, `amount` passed straight to `tx-svc`. Missing type/range checks allow negative amount (reverse transfer), non-numeric, huge values, accounts outside org. Fix: schema-validate (zod/ajv), assert `amount > 0`, integer minor-units, `from`/`to` belong to `:org_id`, `from !== to`.

4. No idempotency key — transfers are money movement. Retries on network blips double-charge. Fix: require `Idempotency-Key` header, forward to `tx-svc`, reject missing with `400`.

5. No error handling / `next(err)` — Section 11. `await txSvc.transfer` rejection escapes to default Express handler, bypasses `src/edge/errorHandler.ts`, may leak stack/internal info in response, violates Section 2 rule 6 (RFC 7807). Fix: wrap in try/catch and `next(err)`, or use async wrapper.

6. Response not RFC 7807 and missing `request_id` on errors — Section 2 rule 6. `{ok:true}` is fine on success but error path returns default Express HTML. Fix: route all errors through central handler producing `problem+json` with `request_id`.

7. Rate limit config not declared for route — Section 4. New route prefix `/v1/orgs/:org_id/transfer` has no entry in `config/rate_limits.json`; falls back to generic 30/min POST. Money endpoint warrants stricter per-user+per-org limit. Fix: add explicit entry, key by user+org not just IP.

8. CODEOWNERS / review gate — Section 6. Touches auth-adjacent trust boundary; ensure change goes through human approval path even though files listed are auth/rate/header specific, a new money route should require security review.

9. Audit log gap — Section 3 step 9, Section 9. Money movement must land in the 365-day audit bucket with `jti`, `user_id`, `org_id`, `from`, `to`, `amount`, `request_id`. Default `audit_log` middleware logs request metadata but handler should emit a transfer-specific audit event. Fix: explicit `logger.audit(...)` call pre/post `tx-svc` call.

10. PII/secret logging risk — Section 2 rule 5, Section 11. If `txSvc.transfer` throws with body echoed, account numbers may hit logs. Fix: ensure `from`/`to` go through `pii_redact` classifier or are treated as sensitive; never log full body on error.

11. `org_id` path param not validated — Section 12. Untyped string passed to `tx-svc`; SQL/lookup injection risk downstream, and mismatch with JWT `org` claim not enforced (see #2). Fix: validate UUID/format, compare to `req.user.org`.

12. No `body_size_guard` concern but no content-type check — handler assumes `req.body` parsed; if `express.json()` not applied or wrong content-type, `req.body` is `undefined` and destructure throws `TypeError`. Fix: enforce `Content-Type: application/json`, reject otherwise with `415`.

1. Trusts client `X-User-Id` header for identity — Section 2 rule 1, Section 5. Client-supplied `x-user-id` is stripped at edge; even if not, it is forbidden as identity source. Attacker sets any user. Fix: read `req.user.sub` (or `X-Internal-User-Id` set after `auth_jwt`); remove `req.headers['x-user-id']`.

2. No authentication/authorization on route — Section 3, Section 5. Handler registered with `app.post` bypasses pipeline expectations; no check that caller's JWT `org` claim matches `:org_id`, no role check for transfer. Cross-org fund movement possible. Fix: mount under the authed router, assert `req.user.org === req.params.org_id` (or membership lookup) and required role in `req.user.roles`.

3. No input validation on body — Section 12 (correctness), Section 2 (trust boundary). `from`, `to`, `amount` passed straight to `tx-svc`. Missing type/range checks allow negative amount (reverse transfer), non-numeric, huge values, accounts outside org. Fix: schema-validate (zod/ajv), assert `amount > 0`, integer minor-units, `from`/`to` belong to `:org_id`, `from !== to`.

4. No idempotency key — transfers are money movement. Retries on network blips double-charge. Fix: require `Idempotency-Key` header, forward to `tx-svc`, reject missing with `400`.

5. No error handling / `next(err)` — Section 11. `await txSvc.transfer` rejection escapes to default Express handler, bypasses `src/edge/errorHandler.ts`, may leak stack/internal info in response, violates Section 2 rule 6 (RFC 7807). Fix: wrap in try/catch and `next(err)`, or use async wrapper.

6. Response not RFC 7807 and missing `request_id` on errors — Section 2 rule 6. `{ok:true}` is fine on success but error path returns default Express HTML. Fix: route all errors through central handler producing `problem+json` with `request_id`.

7. Rate limit config not declared for route — Section 4. New route prefix `/v1/orgs/:org_id/transfer` has no entry in `config/rate_limits.json`; falls back to generic 30/min POST. Money endpoint warrants stricter per-user+per-org limit. Fix: add explicit entry, key by user+org not just IP.

8. CODEOWNERS / review gate — Section 6. Touches auth-adjacent trust boundary; ensure change goes through human approval path even though files listed are auth/rate/header specific, a new money route should require security review.

9. Audit log gap — Section 3 step 9, Section 9. Money movement must land in the 365-day audit bucket with `jti`, `user_id`, `org_id`, `from`, `to`, `amount`, `request_id`. Default `audit_log` middleware logs request metadata but handler should emit a transfer-specific audit event. Fix: explicit `logger.audit(...)` call pre/post `tx-svc` call.

10. PII/secret logging risk — Section 2 rule 5, Section 11. If `txSvc.transfer` throws with body echoed, account numbers may hit logs. Fix: ensure `from`/`to` go through `pii_redact` classifier or are treated as sensitive; never log full body on error.

11. `org_id` path param not validated — Section 12. Untyped string passed to `tx-svc`; SQL/lookup injection risk downstream, and mismatch with JWT `org` claim not enforced (see #2). Fix: validate UUID/format, compare to `req.user.org`.

12. No `body_size_guard` concern but no content-type check — handler assumes `req.body` parsed; if `express.json()` not applied or wrong content-type, `req.body` is `undefined` and destructure throws `TypeError`. Fix: enforce `Content-Type: application/json`, reject otherwise with `415`.
